title: chenolea incana
intro: Chenolea incana is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It is a small, deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chenolea incana include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and treat respiratory problems. Chenolea incana is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, and its essential oil is used in aromatherapy and as a natural preservative.
